zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏多上行结构
zh.content:
当前AVAX价格维持在6.58附近，短线呈震荡偏多结构，整体仍在小级别上行通道中。多头动能温和释放但未加速，需观察突破

MACD维持零轴上方并继续走强，短期动能偏多；价格站上SMA20显示趋势支撑有效。短线维持偏强结构但仍需量能确认

上方阻力位6.63附近，突破将打开上行空间；下方支撑6.55与6.35两级结构。短期区间震荡为主

操作上建议逢回调接多为主，止损设6.55下方，突破6.63可顺势加仓，控制仓位谨慎追高避免高位追涨风险

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is currently trading in a consolidation phase with a mild bullish bias, holding above the 20-day SMA at 6.35389. Price near 6.588 suggests stabilization after a low-volatility move. Structure shows tentative higher lows within a tight intraday range between 6.5577 and 6.6299, reflecting equilibrium between buyers and sellers. The market appears to be coiling, with participants awaiting a clearer directional breakout from this compressed range.

MACD is positive at 0.0464, indicating bullish momentum, though the absence of a signal line limits crossover confirmation. Low 24-hour volatility and the narrow trading band suggest price compression rather than expansion. Without RSI data, momentum assessment depends primarily on price behavior and MACD direction, which still leans upward but lacks strong conviction. This environment typically precedes either a breakout or continued sideways rotation.

Immediate resistance lies near 6.63, aligned with the recent high of 6.6299, with extension toward 6.65–6.70 if momentum strengthens. Support is anchored at the 20-day SMA around 6.35, acting as a dynamic floor for short-term structure. A deeper breakdown would expose 6.20 as a psychological area where dip buyers may return more aggressively, especially if broader market sentiment remains stable.

Trading conditions favor range strategies in the near term, with dip-buying opportunities near 6.35 support and profit-taking into 6.63 resistance. Risk management is important due to compressed volatility, with tight stops below 6.30 to avoid breakdown risk. A decisive breakout above 6.65 would signal renewed bullish continuation, potentially shifting AVAX into a higher trading range and attracting momentum-driven participation.
